index.description: A comma-separated list of data streams, indices, and index aliases that limit the request. Wildcard expressions (`*`) are supported. To target all data streams and indices in a cluster, omit this parameter or use _all or `*`.

expand_wildcards.description: Expand wildcard expression to concrete indices that are open, closed or both.

level.description: Return health information at a specific level of detail.

local.description: If true, retrieve information from the local node only. If false, retrieve information from the master node.

master_timeout.description: The period to wait for a connection to the master node. If no response is received before the timeout expires, the request fails and returns an error.

timeout.description: The period to wait for a response. If no response is received before the timeout expires, the request fails and returns an error.

wait_for_active_shards.description: Wait for the specified number of active shards. Use `all` to wait for all shards in the cluster to be active. Use `0` to not wait.

wait_for_events.description: Wait until all currently queued events with the given priority are processed.

wait_for_nodes.description: Wait until the specified number (N) of nodes is available. It also accepts `>=N`, `<=N`, `>N` and `<N`. Alternatively, use the notations `ge(N)`, `le(N)`, `gt(N)`, and `lt(N)`.

wait_for_no_initializing_shards.description: Wait (until the timeout expires) for the cluster to have no shard initializations. If false, the request does not wait for initializing shards.

wait_for_no_relocating_shards.description: Wait (until the timeout expires) for the cluster to have no shard relocations. If false, the request not wait for relocating shards.

wait_for_status.description: Wait (until the timeout expires) for the cluster to reach a specific health status (or a better status). A green status is better than yellow and yellow is better than red. By default, the request does not wait for a particular status.
